hypothesis: Systems-optimizer recovery becomes safer if `state.last_rejected_change` must stay backlog-linked and contract-identical to the rejected ratchet card, rather than only preserving the learning text.
measurement: Before the ratchet, a synthetic falsification that drifted `state.last_rejected_change.keep_rule` away from backlog truth still verified clean because `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` only checked `learning_item` alignment for rejected history. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any `last_rejected_change` whose bounded contract fields or `learning_item` drift from the rejected backlog card, while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if `state.last_rejected_change` must stay backlog-linked and contract-identical to one rejected backlog card and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Rejected systems-optimizer ratchets must mirror backlog contract fields in state, or recovery can carry stale failure history that no longer matches the ratchet actually rejected.
